candidate_sets.goal: establish a bounded first public control baseline
candidate_sets.why_bounded_this_way: public history first needs routing, role clarity, and a stable corpus map

candidate_sets.goal: bound interpretation before later authority layers appear
candidate_sets.why_bounded_this_way: early public history becomes easier to read when confusion and citation guardrails are visible

candidate_sets.goal: expose what the corpus claims, how mature those claims are, and how strongly they should be read
candidate_sets.why_bounded_this_way: claim carriers read more cleanly once routing, anti-confusion, and citation posture already exist

candidate_sets.goal: fix canonical home, governing-source order, and invariant protection
candidate_sets.why_bounded_this_way: authority and boundary discipline should appear together rather than as isolated later patches

candidate_sets.goal: make outward-safe routing and bounded handoff logic explicit
candidate_sets.why_bounded_this_way: outward-facing logic is clearest after earlier orientation, claims, and authority framing are already visible

candidate_sets.goal: make local/public cutover discipline explicit once earlier supports already exist
candidate_sets.why_bounded_this_way: policy, atomicity, and sequence are more legible after foundational, interpretive, and boundary framing is already public

candidate_sets.goal: show deliberate incompleteness without making the first public step conceptually noisy
candidate_sets.why_bounded_this_way: non-closure reads best once earlier framing already shows what is claimed, bounded, and routable

anti_patterns.problem: first public cutover includes too many unrelated control layers
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: the first public step becomes an undifferentiated mass of routing, claims, boundaries, and promotion logic
anti_patterns.why_harmful: reviewers cannot tell what the initial public baseline was supposed to establish

anti_patterns.problem: anti-confusion surfaces missing from early public history
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: later public layers appear before distinctions, objections, and citation guardrails are visible
anti_patterns.why_harmful: readers inherit later control wording without the framing needed to interpret it safely

anti_patterns.problem: claims/status exported before their interpretive guardrails
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: claims and maturity classes appear before citation and anti-confusion context
anti_patterns.why_harmful: public history starts sounding stronger or clearer than the visible guardrails justify

anti_patterns.problem: public-safe/export surfaces published without boundary context
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: outward bundle logic appears before safety classes are explicit
anti_patterns.why_harmful: readers may infer that export logic is broader or safer than the corpus actually established

anti_patterns.problem: promotion-control surfaces published before enough earlier framing exists
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: policy, atomicity, or sequencing language appears before the public baseline they depend on
anti_patterns.why_harmful: public history can look procedurally sophisticated while still conceptually under-framed

anti_patterns.problem: open-question layer published without earlier claim/status framing
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: explicit non-closure appears before the corpus shows what is already claimed and how mature it is
anti_patterns.why_harmful: later readers cannot tell whether openness is deliberate restraint or just missing structure

anti_patterns.problem: one repo appears fully current while adjacent repos still lack minimal routing
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: public history looks lopsided across repos and the stack starts reading as unevenly canonized
anti_patterns.why_harmful: cross-repo readers lose the bounded corpus map and start over-reading whichever repo looks most complete

anti_patterns.problem: candidate set treated as command to publish immediately
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: bounded grouping guidance is mistaken for actual release authorization
anti_patterns.why_harmful: planning language gets misread as execution, readiness, or promise

fail_closed_rule: If maintainers cannot determine whether a local layer belongs in an earlier or later candidate set, default to the smaller earlier bounded set and delay wider promotion.
